text.title: Spring @Async Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Spring `@Async` methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every `@Async` method MUST name its executor explicitly: `@Async("emailExecutor")`. Plain `@Async` is forbidden. Each named executor lives in `@Configuration` as a `ThreadPoolTaskExecutor` bean.
rule.rationale: The default executor changes between Boot versions (SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor in Boot 3.2+ with virtual threads enabled); unnamed `@Async` silently picks up the wrong pool.

rule.statement: Every `ThreadPoolTaskExecutor` MUST set `RejectedExecutionHandler` to `CallerRunsPolicy`. Queue capacity MUST be bounded explicitly.
rule.rationale: The default `AbortPolicy` throws under load; `CallerRunsPolicy` slows the caller thread instead of dropping work or unbounded growing the queue.

rule.statement: `@Async` methods MUST return `CompletableFuture<T>` (or `Mono`/`Flux` on reactive stacks). `void` and legacy `Future<T>` returns are forbidden. If `void` is necessary, register an `AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler`.
rule.rationale: `void` swallows exceptions; `Future` lacks composition; `CompletableFuture` lets callers observe failures and chain (`thenCombine`, `allOf`).

rule.statement: Executors MUST install a `TaskDecorator` that propagates MDC, `SecurityContext`, and OpenTelemetry baggage. Async tasks running with empty MDC are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Async threads start with empty `ThreadLocal`; without a decorator, request-id and authenticated principal vanish.

rule.statement: Executors MUST set `setWaitForTasksToCompleteOnShutdown(true)` + `setAwaitTerminationSeconds(30)`. Without graceful shutdown, JVM termination aborts in-flight tasks.
rule.rationale: Silent data loss on email / audit / webhook tasks during deploys.

rule.statement: `@Async` methods MUST NOT be called from another method on the same bean (`this.foo()`). Inject a separate bean or use `AopContext.currentProxy()` with `@EnableAspectJAutoProxy(exposeProxy = true)`. `@Async` MUST NOT be applied inside a `@Transactional` boundary that expects the outer transaction to propagate.
rule.rationale: Self-invocation bypasses the proxy; the call runs synchronously without warning. `@Transactional` does not propagate across a thread boundary.
